key_points: Mertz popular for freshmen; strong floor community reported
Baumhart Hall downtown praised for views, no forced meal plan
Res life reluctant to move you even with serious roommate issues
key_quotes: Living in Baumhart was the best year of my life
The only downfall of Mertz was the elevator rush hour. 10/10 would recommend
From my personal experience, res life is reluctant to escalate to a room change

key_points: School is 70% female; nicknamed 'Gayola' by students
Making friends is genuinely hard, especially for commuters
Introverted culture — 'everyone just minds their own, no one really tryna link'
key_quotes: everyone's too focused on wearing headphones and/or watching their phones. Nobody cares to socialize
I was a commuter senior who lives a solid ways from campus. I had the same issue and it was tough
Lot of queer people and women - the school is 70% female

key_points: Students complain of repetitive, burger-heavy on-campus options
Meal plan system (Rambler Bucks vs dining dollars) confuses students
Rogers Park neighborhood has excellent cheap off-campus food options nearby
key_quotes: Sick of the burgers on campus and snacks
Loyola took away all the public panini presses because everyone would stick their cookies in them
Go down Devon and find a Colombian, Dominican, or Mexican place. You are surrounded by great food

key_points: Wellness Center cannot diagnose ADHD or psychotic symptoms — referrals only
ADHD evaluation is expensive and not handled on campus
Students openly post about depression, hallucinations, and financial stress with limited support
key_quotes: too scared to call them and ask but would the wellness center be able to do anything for a student with auditory and visual hallucinations
It's a lengthy and can be expensive process. The wellness center would probably give you a referral
the transfer was hard and I was really depressed and even had thoughts of harm

key_points: Merit scholarships erode over time due to tuition hikes of $4-6k per year
$186k for nursing BSN after scholarship called 'systematic servitude' by students
Financial aid office reportedly unhelpful; work-study students have far more job options
key_quotes: Your $32k scholarship will only be worth $20k-$22k by the time you're a senior because of tuition increases
$186,000 at 8.05% is honestly insane. Even if you're making $80k-$95k as a BSN, that monthly payment is going to feel like a mortgage
I only got 5500 from FAFSA

key_points: Core curriculum described as 'too rigorous' even by alumni who landed at UChicago grad school
Nursing program consistently praised; NCLEX pass rates and job placement strong
CS program is non-ABET and considered weak; Information Systems is better
key_quotes: I still feel Loyola's core curriculum is too rigorous for a BA
I graduated from LUC nursing and loved it so so much. I passed my NCLEX with the minimum questions
I definitely recommend Loyola, but it's not the best for comp sci people honestly — our program isn't that good

key_points: Advising office widely mocked — unhelpful during registration week, high advisor turnover
Transfer credits handled poorly; courses often downgraded to generic credits
Instructor strike disrupted finals with no clear plan communicated to students
key_quotes: the advising office here is so useless it's hilarious
Loyola likes to protect their credit integrity, a lot of my transfer credits were just generic credits
my advisor just sent an email that she's not going to be working at Loyola anymore

key_points: Lakefront campus is genuinely stunning; lake views a consistent highlight
Jet airplane noise over campus has worsened significantly in recent years
Rogers Park neighborhood is walkable with diverse cheap food; Red Line access to all of Chicago
key_quotes: It used to be one of the most beautiful and quiet campuses in the country — and then the jets started flying directly over campus almost 24/7
The campus, the blue sky, and the lake view looks awesome
lake. beach. study views. public transit to get anywhere you want

key_points: Starting salaries low ($38-48k range); long-term growth reported as solid
Alumni cite LUC as 'not worth the extra cost' for career outcomes vs price
Nursing and finance alumni report strong outcomes; arts and social work less clear
key_quotes: Career-wise would say LUC was not worth the extra cost
In STEM, graduated with ~60k debt. $600 of my income a month goes to private loans
I'm ~10 years post grad and all my nursing friends from Loyola are wildly successful

key_points: Overwhelming student consensus: only worth it with significant scholarship
OOS students paying near-sticker told to look elsewhere by current students
Comparable outcomes available at UIC or DePaul for far less money
key_quotes: It is not worth it. I am only here because I go for free
Don't do it. Your $32k scholarship will only be worth $20k-$22k by the time you're a senior
loyola is def not worth it. even other schools in Chicago like UIC and DePaul beat us in most things

key_points: Beautiful campus, great city access, but social scene requires real effort
Strong Jesuit identity and community; progressive and LGBTQ-friendly atmosphere
Many students genuinely happy here, but commuters and transfers often feel left out
key_quotes: Great diverse student population. Best campus. Amazing location. People like to complain about lack of parties but I've found these people make no effort
People even call us 'Gayola' if that helps too lol
I didn't do well at multiple public schools and Loyola was a game changer for me

key_points: Safety: multiple students shot and killed near campus in Rogers Park
Financial: tuition rises $4-6k/year, eroding scholarships significantly over 4 years
Advising: high advisor turnover, unresponsive during critical registration periods
key_quotes: The gun violence in Chicago does make me nervous I have to admit. What a tragedy
This is not far from where the Duck Walk Killer shot and killed Eliyahu Moscowitz back in 2018
my advisor just sent an email that she's not going to be working at Loyola anymore

key_points: Baumhart Hall downtown gives city-living experience with no forced meal plan
Rogers Park restaurant scene — Taste of Peru, Tamashi Ramen, Morse Gyros — is exceptional
Campus Ministry runs retreats open to non-religious students; strong transfer community builder
key_quotes: Living in Baumhart was the best year of my life. It gives you such a unique opportunity to explore the city
I'm begging you to go to Taste of Peru
Campus Ministry runs a soup kitchen and retreats, numerous involvements with the homeless population; very welcoming
